audit_items.proof_trace: q6adm_alloc_copp contains exactly one allocation (kzalloc at L223). On the pre-allocation error path (L220-221) kzalloc is never called; on the kzalloc-failure path (L224-225) nothing was allocated; on the success path (L234) ownership is unconditionally transferred to the caller. No post-allocation error paths exist because set_bit, field assignments, and init_waitqueue_head are infallible. The caller (q6adm_open, L425) correctly frees the struct via kref_put → q6adm_free_copp (kfree) on any downstream error.
